mapping.name: Pass the Hash
mapping.explanation: Adversaries may “pass the hash” using stolen password hashes to move laterally within an environment, bypassing normal system access controls.

mapping.name: Brute Force
mapping.explanation:
Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.(Citation: TrendMicro Pawn Storm Dec 2020) Without knowledge of the password for an account or set of accounts, an adversary may systematically guess the password using a repetitive or iterative mechanism.(Citation: Dragos Crashoverride 2018) Brute forcing passwords can take place via interaction with a service that will check the validity of those credentials or offline against previously acquired credential data, such as password hashes.

Brute forcing credentials may take place at various points during a breach.

attribution.justification: Ember Bear matches 2/2 observed techniques (profile coverage 100%; semantic similarity 0.66).
APT28 matches 2/2 observed techniques (profile coverage 100%; semantic similarity 0.66).
APT41 matches 2/2 observed techniques (profile coverage 100%; semantic similarity 0.64).
FIN5 matches 1/2 observed techniques (profile coverage 50%; semantic similarity 0.64).
HEXANE matches 1/2 observed techniques (profile coverage 50%; semantic similarity 0.67).

note: Attribution is transparent profile retrieval over public ATT&CK group usage — not a trained classifier.
